message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker state

Update beads JSONL with latest issue status changes reflecting
current NTM project progress and task tracking updates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1klou","title":"E2E Tests: Robot Bulk Assign","description":"E2E tests for Robot Bulk Assign with actual sessions.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Basic --from-bv Allocation
1. Create 5 test beads with known priorities/unblocks
2. Spawn test session with 3 agents
3. Call --robot-bulk-assign --from-bv
4. Verify top 3 beads assigned
5. Verify prompts delivered to panes
6. Verify pane output contains bead IDs

### Scenario 2: --dry-run
1. Setup session and beads
2. Call --robot-bulk-assign --from-bv --dry-run
3. Verify planned_assignments present
4. Verify NO prompts sent (panes unchanged)
5. Verify dry_run flag in response

### Scenario 3: Explicit Allocation
1. Spawn session
2. Call --robot-bulk-assign --allocation='{"2":"bd-test1","3":"bd-test2"}'
3. Verify exactly those beads assigned to those panes
4. Verify no strategy applied

### Scenario 4: --skip-panes
1. Spawn session with 5 agents
2. Call --robot-bulk-assign --from-bv --skip-panes=3,4
3. Verify panes 2, 5, 6 assigned
4. Verify panes 3, 4 NOT assigned
5. Verify skipped_panes in response

### Scenario 5: More Beads Than Panes
1. Create 20 test beads
2. Spawn session with 5 agents
3. Call --robot-bulk-assign --from-bv
4. Verify 5 assigned
5. Verify 15 in unassigned_beads list

### Scenario 6: More Panes Than Beads
1. Create 3 test beads
2. Spawn session with 5 agents
3. Call --robot-bulk-assign --from-bv
4. Verify 3 assigned
5. Verify 2 in unassigned_panes list

### Scenario 7: Different Strategies
1. Create beads with varying unblocks/status
2. Test --strategy=impact, --strategy=ready, --strategy=stale
3. Verify each produces different assignment order

## Test Infrastructure

```bash
# Create test beads
br create task --ephemeral "Test bead 1"
br create task --ephemeral "Test bead 2"
...

# Spawn session
ntm spawn test-bulk-assign --cc=3 --no-user

# Cleanup
ntm kill test-bulk-assign
br delete <test-beads> --force
```

## Logging Requirements

Each test must log:
- Test beads created (IDs, priorities, unblocks)
- Session spawn parameters
- Full --robot-bulk-assign response
- Pane content after assignment (verify prompts)
- Timing for each step
- Pass/fail with full context","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:32:05.044420324Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T03:18:43.671726499Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T03:18:43.671675583Z","close_reason":"E2E tests for Robot Bulk Assign implemented. CLI binding added (--robot-bulk-assign, --from-bv, --allocation, --bulk-strategy, --skip-panes). 24 E2E tests created in e2e/robot_bulk_assign_test.go covering dry-run, explicit allocation, skip-panes, strategies, JSON structure, and edge cases. All tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1klou","depends_on_id":"bd-3nkq5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:32:31.445562374Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1klou","depends_on_id":"bd-t9qp4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:32:05.073016256Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1kq4p","title":"Task: WebSocket event adapters for tool systems","description":"## Scope\nPublish events for beads, mail, reservations, scanner, pipeline, approvals, alerts, accounts, and health.\n\n## Implementation\n- Map internal events to WS event types: bead.created/updated/closed/claimed, mail.received/read/acknowledged, reservation.granted/released/conflict/expired, scanner.started/finding/complete, pipeline.started/step_completed/step_failed/complete, approval.requested/resolved, alert.created/dismissed, account.rotated, health.changed.\n- Ensure events include IDs + context fields (session, agent, bead, pane).\n- Hook into existing tool integrations without polling where possible.\n\n## Logging\n- Log event type + entity IDs at publish time.\n\n## Tests\n- Unit: event mapping correctness.\n- Integration: trigger tool actions and verify WS events emitted.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- All listed tool events appear on WS topics.\n- Event payloads include required context fields.\n","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonEagle","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:52:17.885825200Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T01:56:40.312728048Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T01:56:40.312663177Z","close_reason":"WS tool events now subscribable (topic validation + tests)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1kq4p","depends_on_id":"bd-15amw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:57:57Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kq4p","depends_on_id":"bd-2vzaq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:57:57Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kq4p","depends_on_id":"bd-38p9u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:57:57Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kq4p","depends_on_id":"bd-3hsvc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:57:57Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kq4p","depends_on_id":"bd-6z2bf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:57:57Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kq4p","depends_on_id":"bd-excs1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:57:57Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kq4p","depends_on_id":"bd-f8ikz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:57:57Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1ks5x","title":"[EPIC] Fix resilience monitor false-positive restarts (GH #48)","description":"## Context
GitHub issue #48 — deeply researched bug report. The resilience monitor auto-restart feature falsely detects working agents as crashed and injects the full spawn command as literal keystrokes into the running tmux pane. This is a P0 bug because it actively disrupts working agents.

## Root Cause Analysis (3 independent causes)

### Root Cause 1: False-positive crash detection via text pattern matching
detectProcessStatus() in internal/health/health.go (lines 461-483) scans the last 50 lines of pane output for strings like "exit status", "process exited", "connection closed". AI coding agents routinely output these strings when discussing processes or debugging, triggering ProcessExited while the agent is actively working.

### Root Cause 2: No PID-alive guard before handleCrash()
The resilience monitor in internal/resilience/monitor.go (lines 314-329) calls handleCrash() immediately upon detecting StatusError or ProcessExited without first checking whether the agent process is actually running via PID. By contrast, --robot-smart-restart in internal/robot/smart_restart.go (lines 268-271) already has a guard.

### Root Cause 3: Restart uses send-keys into live pane
restartAgent() (monitor.go line 634) calls sendKeysFn which uses tmux send-keys -l, injecting the full spawn command as literal text into the live pane. For a true crash this is correct (shell is waiting at prompt), but for false positives, keystrokes are received by the running agent, causing chaos.

## Key Insight
NTM already has PID-based liveness checking (internal/robot/status_enrich.go — getChildPID(), getProcessState()) that works correctly, but it is NOT wired into the resilience monitor path. The Pane.PID field is populated from tmux's pane_pid and available in PaneActivity, but checkAgent() at health.go:223 never uses it.

## Current Workaround
Users can set auto_restart = false in ~/.config/ntm/config.toml under [resilience], but false-positive health reporting and crash notifications still fire.

## Affected Files
- internal/health/health.go (lines 223, 461-483)
- internal/resilience/monitor.go (lines 314-329, 596-683)
- internal/robot/health.go (lines 460-530)
- internal/robot/smart_restart.go (lines 268-271, reference for guard)
- internal/robot/status_enrich.go (PID-based checking, to be reused)

## Sub-tasks (see child beads)
1. P0: Extract PID-based liveness checker to shared package (bd-1ks5x.1)
2. P0: Add PID-alive guard before handleCrash() (bd-1ks5x.2)
3. P1: Add consecutive-failures debounce (bd-1ks5x.3)
4. P1: Update checkProcess in robot/health.go (bd-1ks5x.4)
5. P2: Add final PID guard in restartAgent() (bd-1ks5x.5)
6. P1: Comprehensive unit tests (bd-1ks5x.6)
7. P1: E2E test script (bd-1ks5x.7)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:14:42.156985439Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:45:23.437999947Z","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/ntm#48","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["P0","bug","resilience"],"comments":[{"id":254,"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added 2 testing sub-beads: bd-1ks5x.6 (comprehensive unit tests for all new/modified code across process, health, resilience, robot packages) and bd-1ks5x.7 (E2E test script with 4 scenarios: false-positive prevention, real crash detection, debounce verification, final PID guard). Unit tests blocked by all 5 implementation tasks. E2E tests blocked by unit tests.","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:42:49Z"},{"id":263,"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DESCRIPTION PRECISION FIX: Root Cause 1 says detectProcessStatus scans the last 50 lines of pane output. Imprecise: detectProcessStatus receives a pre-captured output string and pattern-matches it for keywords. The 50-line limit is set by the CALLER checkAgent which invokes tmux.CapturePaneOutputContext(ctx, pa.Pane.ID, 50). The function itself has no concept of line count. More accurate: detectProcessStatus in health.go:461-483 pattern-matches the pane output (last 50 lines captured by checkAgent via CapturePaneOutputContext) for strings like exit status, process exited, connection closed.","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:45:23Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1ks5x","title":"[EPIC] Fix resilience monitor false-positive restarts (GH #48)","description":"## Context
GitHub issue #48 — deeply researched bug report. The resilience monitor auto-restart feature falsely detects working agents as crashed and injects the full spawn command as literal keystrokes into the running tmux pane. This is a P0 bug because it actively disrupts working agents.

## Root Cause Analysis (3 independent causes)

### Root Cause 1: False-positive crash detection via text pattern matching
detectProcessStatus() in internal/health/health.go (lines 461-483) scans the last 50 lines of pane output for strings like "exit status", "process exited", "connection closed". AI coding agents routinely output these strings when discussing processes or debugging, triggering ProcessExited while the agent is actively working.

### Root Cause 2: No PID-alive guard before handleCrash()
The resilience monitor in internal/resilience/monitor.go (lines 314-329) calls handleCrash() immediately upon detecting StatusError or ProcessExited without first checking whether the agent process is actually running via PID. By contrast, --robot-smart-restart in internal/robot/smart_restart.go (lines 268-271) already has a guard.

### Root Cause 3: Restart uses send-keys into live pane
restartAgent() (monitor.go line 634) calls sendKeysFn which uses tmux send-keys -l, injecting the full spawn command as literal text into the live pane. For a true crash this is correct (shell is waiting at prompt), but for false positives, keystrokes are received by the running agent, causing chaos.

## Key Insight
NTM already has PID-based liveness checking (internal/robot/status_enrich.go — getChildPID(), getProcessState()) that works correctly, but it is NOT wired into the resilience monitor path. The Pane.PID field is populated from tmux's pane_pid and available in PaneActivity, but checkAgent() at health.go:223 never uses it.

## Current Workaround
Users can set auto_restart = false in ~/.config/ntm/config.toml under [resilience], but false-positive health reporting and crash notifications still fire.

## Affected Files
- internal/health/health.go (lines 223, 461-483)
- internal/resilience/monitor.go (lines 314-329, 596-683)
- internal/robot/health.go (lines 460-530)
- internal/robot/smart_restart.go (lines 268-271, reference for guard)
- internal/robot/status_enrich.go (PID-based checking, to be reused)

## Sub-tasks (see child beads)
1. P0: Extract PID-based liveness checker to shared package (bd-1ks5x.1)
2. P0: Add PID-alive guard before handleCrash() (bd-1ks5x.2)
3. P1: Add consecutive-failures debounce (bd-1ks5x.3)
4. P1: Update checkProcess in robot/health.go (bd-1ks5x.4)
5. P2: Add final PID guard in restartAgent() (bd-1ks5x.5)
6. P1: Comprehensive unit tests (bd-1ks5x.6)
7. P1: E2E test script (bd-1ks5x.7)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:14:42.156985439Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:17:27.132173446Z","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/ntm#48","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["P0","bug","resilience"],"comments":[{"id":254,"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added 2 testing sub-beads: bd-1ks5x.6 (comprehensive unit tests for all new/modified code across process, health, resilience, robot packages) and bd-1ks5x.7 (E2E test script with 4 scenarios: false-positive prevention, real crash detection, debounce verification, final PID guard). Unit tests blocked by all 5 implementation tasks. E2E tests blocked by unit tests.","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:42:49Z"},{"id":263,"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DESCRIPTION PRECISION FIX: Root Cause 1 says detectProcessStatus scans the last 50 lines of pane output. Imprecise: detectProcessStatus receives a pre-captured output string and pattern-matches it for keywords. The 50-line limit is set by the CALLER checkAgent which invokes tmux.CapturePaneOutputContext(ctx, pa.Pane.ID, 50). The function itself has no concept of line count. More accurate: detectProcessStatus in health.go:461-483 pattern-matches the pane output (last 50 lines captured by checkAgent via CapturePaneOutputContext) for strings like exit status, process exited, connection closed.","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:45:23Z"},{"id":268,"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"KNOWN LIMITATION: After all sub-tasks are implemented, the ntm status CLI (cli/health.go:256,435) and TUI dashboard (tui/dashboard/dashboard.go:2209) will still show false ProcessExited for running agents. These use health.CheckSession which calls text-based detectProcessStatus — NOT PID-based checking. The PID guard only prevents false RESTARTS in the resilience monitor. The PID-based checkProcess (bd-1ks5x.4) only affects the robot path (ntm diagnose). STATUS DISPLAY PATH: ntm status / TUI -> health.CheckSession -> detectProcessStatus (text-only) -> may show false ProcessExited. RESILIENCE PATH: monitor -> health.CheckSession -> PID guard -> correct restart decision. This is P2 follow-up work: either add PID override to health.checkAgent ProcessStatus, or have the monitor update a shared status that the TUI reads. Not blocking for the P0 fix (preventing false restarts is the critical path).","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:17:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ks5x.1","title":"Create PID-based liveness checker (internal/process/liveness.go)","description":"## What
Extract existing PID liveness checking into a shared internal/process package, then wire it into the health check path.

## CRITICAL: Reuse existing code, do NOT duplicate
NTM already has working PID-based liveness code in TWO places:
1. internal/robot/status_enrich.go:107-128 — getChildPID(shellPID int) (int, error) using /proc then pgrep fallback
2. internal/integrations/rano/pidmap.go:271-275 — same /proc/[pid]/task/[tid]/children pattern

The new internal/process package should EXTRACT this logic, and the existing callers should be updated to import from the shared package. Do NOT create a third copy.

## Why (P0)
This is the foundational fix that eliminates false-positive crash detection. The current text-based pattern matching in detectProcessStatus() (health.go:461-483) triggers on agent output containing strings like "exit status" — which AI agents routinely produce. PID-based checking is authoritative: if the shell has a child process, the agent is alive, period.

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Create internal/process/liveness.go
```go
package process

// IsChildAlive checks whether a tmux pane shell PID has at least one
// non-zombie child process. Uses /proc/<pid>/task/<pid>/children (Linux),
// falls back to pgrep -P.
func IsChildAlive(shellPID int) bool

// ChildPIDs returns all non-zombie child PIDs of the given process.
func ChildPIDs(shellPID int) []int

// GetChildPID returns the first non-zombie child PID (the agent process).
// Returns 0, error if no living child found.
func GetChildPID(shellPID int) (int, error)

// GetProcessState reads /proc/<pid>/status for process state info.
// Returns state code (S/R/Z/T/etc), state name, error.
func GetProcessState(pid int) (string, string, error)
```

### Step 2: MANDATORY zombie filtering in IsChildAlive and ChildPIDs
/proc/pid/task/tid/children includes zombie (Z-state) children. A zombie agent is dead from our perspective — it has exited but the parent has not yet waited for it. IsChildAlive MUST filter Z-state children:
```go
func IsChildAlive(shellPID int) bool {
    children := rawChildPIDs(shellPID) // from /proc or pgrep
    for _, childPID := range children {
        state, _, err := GetProcessState(childPID)
        if err != nil {
            continue // process vanished between reads — skip
        }
        if state != "Z" { // NOT a zombie
            return true
        }
    }
    return false
}
```
Without this filtering, a zombied agent would keep IsChildAlive returning true indefinitely, and the agent would NEVER be restarted.

### Step 3: Migrate existing callers
- robot/status_enrich.go:107 — change getChildPID() to call process.GetChildPID()
- robot/status_enrich.go:130 — change getProcessState() to call process.GetProcessState()
- integrations/rano/pidmap.go:271 — use process.ChildPIDs() instead of inline /proc read
- Remove the now-duplicate local functions

### Step 4: Wire into health.go:223
In checkAgent(), before calling detectProcessStatus():
```go
// PID-based liveness check (authoritative)
if pa.Pane.PID > 0 {
    if process.IsChildAlive(pa.Pane.PID) {
        agent.ProcessStatus = ProcessRunning
        // Skip text-based detection entirely — PID says alive
    } else {
        agent.ProcessStatus = ProcessExited
        // Agent process is genuinely dead (or only zombie children)
    }
} else {
    // Fallback to text-based detection (PID unavailable)
    agent.ProcessStatus = detectProcessStatus(output, pa.Pane.Command)
}
```

### Step 5: Retain text-based fallback
Do NOT remove detectProcessStatus(). Keep it as the fallback path for when PID is 0/unavailable. This should be rare with tmux (pane_pid is always populated) but provides defense in depth.

## Key Files
- NEW: internal/process/liveness.go
- NEW: internal/process/liveness_test.go
- MODIFY: internal/health/health.go:176-223 (checkAgent, add PID-based path)
- MODIFY: internal/robot/status_enrich.go:107-148 (delegate to process package)
- MODIFY: internal/integrations/rano/pidmap.go:271 (delegate to process package)

## Edge Cases
- PID = 0: skip PID check, use text fallback (should never happen with tmux)
- /proc unavailable: pgrep fallback handles this
- pgrep unavailable: return error, treat as PID unavailable
- Race condition (child exits during check): acceptable — worst case is one false negative (thinks alive when just died), caught on next health check cycle
- Zombie children: MUST be filtered via GetProcessState(). /proc/pid/children includes zombies, pgrep does not. Without zombie filtering, a zombied agent would appear alive forever and never be restarted.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:15:38.958137839Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:12:40.787700839Z","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/ntm#48/pid-liveness","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["P0","bug","resilience"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1ks5x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:15:38.958137839Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1ks5x.2","title":"Add PID-alive guard before handleCrash() in resilience monitor","description":"## What
Add a PID-based liveness guard in internal/resilience/monitor.go before handleCrash() at lines 314-329. If the agent process is alive (PID check), skip crash handling regardless of what text patterns say.

## Why (P0 — defense in depth)
The resilience monitor currently calls handleCrash() immediately upon detecting StatusError or ProcessExited with NO check for whether the agent process is actually running. The robot path (smart_restart.go:268-271) already has an IsWorking guard. The resilience monitor needs the same protection, but using the more authoritative PID-based check.

## Important: This is a PID-alive check, NOT the full IsWorking analysis
The robot path IsWorking field is a composite signal (output analysis, context window, prompt detection). For the resilience monitor, we use a simpler, more authoritative check: process.IsChildAlive(shellPID). If the shell has a child process, the agent is alive and we must NOT restart it. This is a hard gate, not a heuristic.

## Implementation

### Step 1: Add ShellPID to AgentHealth struct
In internal/health/health.go, add to AgentHealth:
```go
ShellPID int json:"shell_pid" // Shell PID from tmux #{pane_pid}
```

### Step 2: Populate ShellPID in checkAgent()
In checkAgent() (health.go:176), add:
```go
agent.ShellPID = pa.Pane.PID
```

### Step 3: Add guard in monitor.go
At lines 314-329, before handleCrash():
```go
// PID-alive guard: if agent process is running, skip crash handling
if agentHealth.ShellPID > 0 && process.IsChildAlive(agentHealth.ShellPID) {
    log.Printf("[resilience] Agent %s: health check says unhealthy but PID %d has children — skipping crash handling (false positive avoided)", agentState.PaneID, agentHealth.ShellPID)
    continue
}
```

### Step 4: Add import
Add `"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/process"` to monitor.go imports.

## Key Files
- MODIFY: internal/health/health.go (AgentHealth struct + checkAgent())
- MODIFY: internal/resilience/monitor.go (lines 314-329, add guard + import)
- REFERENCE: internal/robot/smart_restart.go:268-271 (analogous guard)
- REFERENCE: internal/process/liveness.go (from bd-1ks5x.1)

## Behavior Matrix
| PID Check | Text Check | Result |
|-----------|-----------|--------|
| alive     | any       | SKIP crash (PID is authoritative) |
| dead      | error     | proceed to handleCrash (after debounce, see bd-1ks5x.3) |
| dead      | ok        | proceed to handleCrash (PID says dead) |
| unknown   | error     | proceed to handleCrash (after debounce) |
| unknown   | ok        | mark healthy |","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:15:42.379594439Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:30.358986787Z","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/ntm#48/isworking-guard","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["P0","bug","resilience"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1ks5x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:15:42.379594439Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1ks5x.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:15:59.131595764Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":257,"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CLARIFICATION: ShellPID is added to AgentHealth (this bead) AND AgentState (bd-1ks5x.5). This is intentional — AgentHealth is the per-check-cycle result struct consumed by the monitor loop, while AgentState is the persistent per-session struct used in restartAgent(). Both need ShellPID because they operate in different code paths with different lifecycles.","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:13:49Z"},{"id":262,"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IMPLEMENTATION ORDER NOTE: bd-1ks5x.2 adds the guard with its own IsChildAlive call. Then bd-1ks5x.3 REFACTORS the guard into the single-call pattern (pidAlive/pidKnown variables shared between guard and debounce). When implementing bd-1ks5x.3, the guard code from bd-1ks5x.2 is REPLACED, not augmented.

DOUBLE ISCHILDALIVE CALL (INTENTIONAL): After both bd-1ks5x.1 and bd-1ks5x.2/3 are implemented, IsChildAlive is called TWICE per check cycle: once in health.go (to set ProcessStatus) and once in monitor.go (guard/debounce). This is intentional defense-in-depth — the health.go call determines status for the TUI/API, while the monitor.go call is the resilience monitor's independent verification before taking action (restart). The cost is one extra /proc read per cycle (~microseconds).","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:26:32Z"},{"id":265,"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BEHAVIOR MATRIX ROW UNREACHABLE: The row 'dead | ok -> proceed to handleCrash (PID says dead)' cannot happen in the proposed implementation. The PID guard code goes INSIDE the existing 'if StatusError || ProcessExited' block. If text check says ok (neither StatusError nor ProcessExited), execution goes to the else branch (mark healthy). The PID check is never reached. To implement this row, we would need to restructure the outer if/else to check PID FIRST regardless of text patterns. This is a bigger change. PRACTICAL IMPACT: Minimal. If PID is dead, the agent exited and the shell is at a prompt. The NEXT check cycle text patterns will detect the shell prompt and trigger the error path normally. One-cycle delay (15s). RECOMMENDATION: Remove the dead|ok row from the matrix or add a comment that it is aspirational for a future enhancement where PID check is promoted above the text-based condition.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:30Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1ks5x.2","title":"Add PID-alive guard before handleCrash() in resilience monitor","description":"## What
Add a PID-based liveness guard in internal/resilience/monitor.go before handleCrash() at lines 314-329. If the agent process is alive (PID check), skip crash handling regardless of what text patterns say.

## Why (P0 — defense in depth)
The resilience monitor currently calls handleCrash() immediately upon detecting StatusError or ProcessExited with NO check for whether the agent process is actually running. The robot path (smart_restart.go:268-271) already has an IsWorking guard. The resilience monitor needs the same protection, but using the more authoritative PID-based check.

## Important: This is a PID-alive check, NOT the full IsWorking analysis
The robot path IsWorking field is a composite signal (output analysis, context window, prompt detection). For the resilience monitor, we use a simpler, more authoritative check: process.IsChildAlive(shellPID). If the shell has a child process, the agent is alive and we must NOT restart it. This is a hard gate, not a heuristic.

## Implementation

### Step 1: Add ShellPID to AgentHealth struct
In internal/health/health.go, add to AgentHealth:
```go
ShellPID int json:"shell_pid" // Shell PID from tmux #{pane_pid}
```

### Step 2: Populate ShellPID in checkAgent()
In checkAgent() (health.go:176), add:
```go
agent.ShellPID = pa.Pane.PID
```

### Step 3: Add guard in monitor.go
At lines 314-329, before handleCrash():
```go
// PID-alive guard: if agent process is running, skip crash handling
if agentHealth.ShellPID > 0 && process.IsChildAlive(agentHealth.ShellPID) {
    log.Printf("[resilience] Agent %s: health check says unhealthy but PID %d has children — skipping crash handling (false positive avoided)", agentState.PaneID, agentHealth.ShellPID)
    continue
}
```

### Step 4: Add import
Add `"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/process"` to monitor.go imports.

## Key Files
- MODIFY: internal/health/health.go (AgentHealth struct + checkAgent())
- MODIFY: internal/resilience/monitor.go (lines 314-329, add guard + import)
- REFERENCE: internal/robot/smart_restart.go:268-271 (analogous guard)
- REFERENCE: internal/process/liveness.go (from bd-1ks5x.1)

## Behavior Matrix
| PID Check | Text Check | Result |
|-----------|-----------|--------|
| alive     | any       | SKIP crash (PID is authoritative) |
| dead      | error     | proceed to handleCrash (after debounce, see bd-1ks5x.3) |
| dead      | ok        | proceed to handleCrash (PID says dead) |
| unknown   | error     | proceed to handleCrash (after debounce) |
| unknown   | ok        | mark healthy |","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:15:42.379594439Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:21:09.149279755Z","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/ntm#48/isworking-guard","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["P0","bug","resilience"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1ks5x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:15:42.379594439Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1ks5x.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:15:59.131595764Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":257,"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CLARIFICATION: ShellPID is added to AgentHealth (this bead) AND AgentState (bd-1ks5x.5). This is intentional — AgentHealth is the per-check-cycle result struct consumed by the monitor loop, while AgentState is the persistent per-session struct used in restartAgent(). Both need ShellPID because they operate in different code paths with different lifecycles.","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:13:49Z"},{"id":262,"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IMPLEMENTATION ORDER NOTE: bd-1ks5x.2 adds the guard with its own IsChildAlive call. Then bd-1ks5x.3 REFACTORS the guard into the single-call pattern (pidAlive/pidKnown variables shared between guard and debounce). When implementing bd-1ks5x.3, the guard code from bd-1ks5x.2 is REPLACED, not augmented.

DOUBLE ISCHILDALIVE CALL (INTENTIONAL): After both bd-1ks5x.1 and bd-1ks5x.2/3 are implemented, IsChildAlive is called TWICE per check cycle: once in health.go (to set ProcessStatus) and once in monitor.go (guard/debounce). This is intentional defense-in-depth — the health.go call determines status for the TUI/API, while the monitor.go call is the resilience monitor's independent verification before taking action (restart). The cost is one extra /proc read per cycle (~microseconds).","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:26:32Z"},{"id":265,"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BEHAVIOR MATRIX ROW UNREACHABLE: The row 'dead | ok -> proceed to handleCrash (PID says dead)' cannot happen in the proposed implementation. The PID guard code goes INSIDE the existing 'if StatusError || ProcessExited' block. If text check says ok (neither StatusError nor ProcessExited), execution goes to the else branch (mark healthy). The PID check is never reached. To implement this row, we would need to restructure the outer if/else to check PID FIRST regardless of text patterns. This is a bigger change. PRACTICAL IMPACT: Minimal. If PID is dead, the agent exited and the shell is at a prompt. The NEXT check cycle text patterns will detect the shell prompt and trigger the error path normally. One-cycle delay (15s). RECOMMENDATION: Remove the dead|ok row from the matrix or add a comment that it is aspirational for a future enhancement where PID check is promoted above the text-based condition.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:30Z"},{"id":267,"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CORRECTION TO COMMENT 2: The statement 'IsChildAlive is called TWICE: once in health.go (to set ProcessStatus)' is wrong. health/health.go checkAgent does NOT call IsChildAlive — it only stores ShellPID as a pass-through (this bead Step 2). The PID-based ProcessStatus check is in robot/health.go checkProcess (bd-1ks5x.4), a DIFFERENT file in a DIFFERENT package. Correct statement: IsChildAlive is called twice per cycle: once in robot/health.go checkProcess (for TUI/diagnose commands via bd-1ks5x.4), and once in resilience/monitor.go (for the auto-restart guard via this bead). The internal/health package does NOT import or call process.IsChildAlive.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:21:09Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ks5x.3","title":"Add consecutive-failures debounce to resilience monitor","description":"## What
Add ConsecutiveFailures counter to AgentState. Only trigger handleCrash() after N consecutive health check failures (default N=3, configurable). Integrate with the PID-alive guard result from bd-1ks5x.2 to avoid redundant IsChildAlive calls.

## Why (P1)
The debounce is specifically for the TEXT-BASED FALLBACK path (when PID checking is unavailable or returns unknown). For PID-authoritative signals, the behavior is:
- PID alive → always skip (handled by guard in bd-1ks5x.2, never reaches debounce)
- PID dead → immediate restart (no debounce, authoritative)
- PID unknown + text patterns say error → DEBOUNCE (unreliable signal needs temporal smoothing)

This ensures fast response to genuine crashes (PID dead = instant) while protecting against text pattern false positives in the rare case where PID checking is unavailable.

## CRITICAL: Single IsChildAlive call, shared result
The PID-alive guard (bd-1ks5x.2) calls process.IsChildAlive() ONCE. The result MUST be stored and passed to the debounce logic. Do NOT call IsChildAlive a second time — this creates a TOCTOU race where the child could die between the two calls, leading to inconsistent decisions.

Pattern:
```go
// In the monitor loop, SINGLE PID check, reuse result
var pidAlive, pidKnown bool
if agentHealth.ShellPID > 0 {
    pidAlive = process.IsChildAlive(agentHealth.ShellPID)
    pidKnown = true
}

// PID-alive guard (bd-1ks5x.2)
if pidKnown && pidAlive {
    continue // agent running, skip crash handling
}

// Debounce (this bead)
if pidKnown && !pidAlive {
    // PID authoritatively dead — skip debounce, restart immediately
    agentState.ConsecutiveFailures = m.cfg.Resilience.CrashThreshold
} else if !pidKnown {
    // PID unavailable, text-based fallback — increment debounce counter
    agentState.ConsecutiveFailures++
}

if agentState.ConsecutiveFailures >= m.cfg.Resilience.CrashThreshold {
    m.handleCrash(ctx, agentState, reason)
    agentState.ConsecutiveFailures = 0
}
```

## Implementation

### Step 1: Add fields to AgentState
```go
ConsecutiveFailures int    // Consecutive health check failures
LastFailureReason   string // For logging
```

### Step 2: Add config field
In config.toml under [resilience]:
```toml
crash_threshold = 3  # Consecutive failures before restart (for text-based fallback path)
```

In internal/config, add:
```go
CrashThreshold int \`toml:"crash_threshold" default:"3"\`
```

### Step 3: Modify monitor loop (lines 314-329)
Replace the current flow with the single-call pattern shown above. The guard and debounce operate on the SAME pidAlive/pidKnown variables.

### Step 4: Reset on healthy
When agent transitions to healthy:
```go
agentState.ConsecutiveFailures = 0
agentState.Healthy = true
```

## Key Design Decision
Debounce does NOT delay PID-dead restarts. If the PID check authoritatively says the agent is dead, we restart immediately (set failures = threshold). The debounce only gates the unreliable text-based path.

## Key Files
- MODIFY: internal/resilience/monitor.go (AgentState, monitor loop lines 314-334)
- MODIFY: internal/config/config.go or relevant config struct (CrashThreshold)
- MODIFY: config.toml template/defaults

## Testing
- threshold=3, PID dead → restart on first check (failures forced to threshold)
- threshold=3, PID unknown + text error → restart on 3rd check
- threshold=3, PID unknown + text error x 2 then healthy → reset to 0, no restart
- threshold=1, PID unknown + text error → restart on first check (no debounce)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:15:46.047204466Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:09:29.240757815Z","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/ntm#48/debounce","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["P1","bug","resilience"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1ks5x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:15:46.047204466Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1ks5x.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:15:59.497066732Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":255,"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RACE CONDITION FIX: The PID-alive guard (bd-1ks5x.2) and debounce (bd-1ks5x.3) should NOT call process.IsChildAlive() independently. The guard checks IsChildAlive first; if it passes (child dead or unknown), store the result in a local variable and pass it to the debounce logic. This avoids a TOCTOU race where the child dies between the guard check and the debounce check. Implementation: the guard call returns a (pidAlive bool, pidKnown bool) tuple; if pidKnown && !pidAlive the debounce immediately sets failures=threshold (skip debounce for authoritative PID-dead). If !pidKnown, increment failures normally (debounce the unreliable text signal).","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:12:49Z"},{"id":260,"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"NESTING FIX: The debounce and PID guard code must go INSIDE the existing 'if agentState.Healthy' block at monitor.go:318, NOT outside it. The current code structure is: if StatusError/ProcessExited { if agentState.Healthy { handleCrash() } }. The guard and debounce replace the inner handleCrash() call within that nesting, NOT the outer condition check.","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:24:17Z"},{"id":264,"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"LOGIC BUG: ConsecutiveFailures not reset when PID-alive guard fires. In the proposed code, when pidKnown and pidAlive, we continue without resetting failures. This creates a slow-burn false restart: Check 1: PID unknown + text error -> failures=1. Check 2: PID alive -> continue (failures stays 1). Check 3: PID unknown + text error -> failures=2. Check 4: PID alive -> continue (stays 2). Check 5: PID unknown + text error -> failures=3 -> RESTART despite being alive in checks 2 and 4. FIX: Reset ConsecutiveFailures in the PID-alive guard path: if pidKnown && pidAlive { agentState.ConsecutiveFailures = 0; continue }. The agent is provably alive so prior failure counts should be wiped.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:09:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ks5x.4","title":"Update checkProcess() in robot/health.go to use PID-based liveness","description":"## What
Apply PID-based liveness checking to the TUI health path at internal/robot/health.go checkProcess() (lines 460-530) and its caller CheckAgentHealthWithActivity() (line 432).

## Why (P1)
The TUI path uses the same false-positive-prone text pattern matching. While it does not auto-restart, it reports incorrect health status in the TUI dashboard causing confusion and false alarms.

## CRITICAL: checkProcess takes paneID, not shellPID
checkProcess(paneID string) currently takes only a pane ID string. It has no access to the shell PID. To add PID-based checking, one of these approaches is needed:

### Recommended approach: Change checkProcess signature
```go
// Before:
func checkProcess(paneID string) *ProcessCheckResult

// After:
func checkProcess(paneID string, shellPID int) *ProcessCheckResult
```

Then update the caller at line 442:
```go
// CheckAgentHealthWithActivity needs shellPID too
func CheckAgentHealthWithActivity(paneID string, agentType string, shellPID int) (*HealthCheck, error) {
    // ...
    check.ProcessCheck = checkProcess(paneID, shellPID)
    // ...
}
```

And update ALL callers of CheckAgentHealthWithActivity:
- robot/health.go:815 — has access to pane.ID and pane.PID (from tmux.ListPanes)
- robot/health.go:2082 — needs pane PID passed in
- robot/diagnose.go:146 — has access to pane.ID and pane.PID
- robot/diagnose.go:479 — has access to pane.ID and pane.PID

### Implementation in checkProcess
At the top of checkProcess, before text pattern matching:
```go
func checkProcess(paneID string, shellPID int) *ProcessCheckResult {
    result := &ProcessCheckResult{Running: true, Crashed: false}

    // PID-based liveness check (authoritative)
    if shellPID > 0 {
        if process.IsChildAlive(shellPID) {
            return result // Running=true, agent is alive
        }
        // PID says dead, but still check text patterns for better diagnostics
        result.Running = false
        result.Crashed = true
        result.Reason = "PID-based check: no living child process"
        // Continue to text patterns to populate ExitStatus with more detail
    }

    // ... existing text pattern matching follows ...
}
```

## Key Files
- MODIFY: internal/robot/health.go (checkProcess signature, CheckAgentHealthWithActivity signature, lines 442, 460-530)
- MODIFY: internal/robot/health.go:815 (caller update)
- MODIFY: internal/robot/health.go:2082 (caller update)
- MODIFY: internal/robot/diagnose.go:146, 479 (caller updates)
- REFERENCE: internal/process/liveness.go (IsChildAlive)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:15:48.764605063Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:13:34.679362925Z","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/ntm#48/robot-health","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["P1","bug","resilience"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1ks5x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:15:48.764605063Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1ks5x.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:15:59.840984055Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ks5x.5","title":"Add final PID guard before sendFunc() in restartAgent()","description":"## What
Add a last-second PID liveness check in restartAgent() at monitor.go ~line 634, just before sendFunc(). If the agent is still alive, abort the restart.

## Why (P2 — final safety net)
The restart path has a delay (RestartDelaySeconds, default 5s) before injecting keys. During this delay, conditions may change — the agent may recover, or a false positive may resolve. A final PID check right before sending keys prevents the most damaging outcome (injecting spawn command as literal keystrokes into a running agent) even if all other guards failed.

## How
1. The shell PID must be available in restartAgent() scope. Currently AgentState has PaneID and PaneIndex but NOT the shell PID. Add ShellPID int to AgentState struct (populated during RegisterAgent() from Pane.PID).
2. Before sendFunc() at line 634:
```go
if agent.ShellPID > 0 && process.IsChildAlive(agent.ShellPID) {
    log.Printf("[resilience] Aborting restart for %s: agent still alive (shell PID %d has children)", agent.PaneID, agent.ShellPID)
    // Mark healthy again since agent is actually running
    m.mu.Lock()
    if a, ok := m.agents[agent.PaneID]; ok {
        a.Healthy = true
    }
    m.mu.Unlock()
    return
}
```
3. Also update RegisterAgent() to populate ShellPID from the Pane struct.

## Key Files
- MODIFY: internal/resilience/monitor.go (AgentState struct + restartAgent() + RegisterAgent())
- REFERENCE: internal/process/liveness.go (IsChildAlive function)

## Edge Cases
- ShellPID = 0: skip the guard (PID unknown, proceed with restart — better than blocking all restarts)
- Race condition: child exits between check and sendFunc. This is acceptable — we gave it our best check. The text injection into an empty shell prompt is the correct behavior for a genuine crash.
- Concurrent restarts: the m.mu.Lock() in restartAgent already serializes access, so no double-restart concern.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:15:52.472138590Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:24:10.376097125Z","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/ntm#48/final-guard","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["P2","bug","resilience"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1ks5x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:15:52.472138590Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1ks5x.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:16:00.203791859Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":256,"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CLARIFICATION: ShellPID in TWO structs is INTENTIONAL, not a conflict. AgentHealth (health.go, bd-1ks5x.2) is the health check RESULT struct — populated per check cycle, consumed by the monitor. AgentState (monitor.go, this bead) is the resilience monitor's PERSISTENT state — populated once during RegisterAgent(), used for the final PID guard in restartAgent(). These are different lifecycle/scope: AgentHealth is ephemeral (per-check), AgentState is long-lived (per-agent-session). Both need ShellPID because they are used in different code paths.","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:13:43Z"},{"id":259,"issue_id":"bd-1ks5x.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IMPLEMENTATION DETAIL: RegisterAgent() at monitor.go:91 takes (paneID, paneIndex, agentType, model, command) — it does NOT take a Pane struct or shellPID. Two changes needed:
1. Add shellPID int parameter to RegisterAgent(): func (m *Monitor) RegisterAgent(paneID string, paneIndex int, agentType, model, command string, shellPID int)
2. ScanAndRegisterAgents() at line 172 already has p.PID from tmux.GetPanes() — just add ShellPID: p.PID to the AgentState initialization.
3. Update ALL callers of RegisterAgent() to pass the shell PID.","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:24:10Z"}]}
